contexts.name: Alert Attribution & Channels
contexts.description: Alerting beyond the raw breach signal: attribute a spike or budget breach to a likely cause (model, project, prompt), and fan the resulting alert out across configured delivery channels.

contexts.name: Trace Trees & Span Scoring
contexts.description: Multi-span trace model above single events: assemble parent/child spans into a trace tree, expose trace query endpoints, render trace views, and let the runner score whole traces rather than isolated generations.

contexts.name: Ingest Hardening & Idempotency
contexts.description: Defensive layer in front of event ingest: batch POST handling, payload validation, idempotency-key dedup so retried writes do not double-count, and structured rejection responses for malformed or over-limit submissions.

contexts.name: Projects & Access Control
contexts.description: Create projects and mint scoped API keys that authorize ingest and reads. Each backend stores the project record plus its keys and redaction policy; the render layer lists projects for operators.

contexts.name: Budget Limits & Breach Alerts
contexts.description: Keep monitored ingest traffic inside per-project budgets: define limit rules (cost/count over rolling windows), evaluate them on every event to set a throttle flag, and fire deduplicated webhook/ntfy alerts off the request path when a budget is breached.

contexts.name: Event Ingestion & Query
contexts.description: Capture every LLM generation as a normalized trace: POST events get cost-computed and limit-checked, then can be queried by project, fetched by id, or rolled into a cost summary. Optional server-side PII scrubbing redacts input/output on the way in.

contexts.name: Prompt Registry & Versioning
contexts.description: Store, version and serve the prompts under evaluation: prompt records in core, CRUD endpoints, SQLite/Firestore persistence, markdown rendering, and MCP tools that let an agent read and write prompts.

contexts.name: Judge Pairwise, Parsing & Provider Pool
contexts.description: Judge engine internals underneath the rubric prompt: pairwise A/B comparison judging, tolerant parsing of model JSON verdicts, a provider connection pool, and retry/backoff around flaky provider calls.

contexts.name: Scoring Rubrics
contexts.description: Author and store the rubrics that drive judging: named dimensions with weights and guidance. The runner can load a rubric for a scoring run; the render layer presents rubric definitions.

contexts.name: Score Recording & Query
contexts.description: Persist and retrieve judge verdicts as scores tied to events or ad-hoc pairs. The runner's score command judges recent unscored events (idempotent, optionally looping), and scores are queryable per project and rendered as tables.

contexts.name: Judge Engine
contexts.description: The LLM-as-judge core: build a structured judging prompt from a rubric, call a provider-configurable model (Claude via CLI, or OpenAI/Gemini over HTTP), and parse its JSON verdict. Includes calibration that measures judge-vs-human agreement.

contexts.name: Calibration Watch & Run Stats
contexts.description: Keep judge quality honest over time: watch calibration drift between judge and human labels, compute run statistics, handle recurring scheduled runs, and gate promotion on statistical thresholds.

contexts.name: Background Job Queue
contexts.description: Run long benchmark work asynchronously: enqueue a job, let a worker claim it, report progress, and finish it (with retry up to max attempts). Jobs are persisted per backend and surfaced for monitoring.

contexts.name: Evaluation Datasets
contexts.description: Build frozen evaluation datasets by sampling and anonymizing live events, add or list items, and freeze them for reproducible benchmarking. The runner can sample on a schedule (daemon or one-shot cron) so idle periods cost nothing.

contexts.name: Benchmark Suites & Runs
contexts.description: Define benchmark suites of cases and execute them: the runner judges each case (rubric or compare mode), aggregates a scorecard, and records a run. Compare mode produces a multi-target leaderboard of quality vs cost vs latency.

contexts.name: Margin Simulation & FX
contexts.description: Model profitability under change: simulate margin against pricing/usage scenarios, track margin trend over time, convert currencies via FX rates, and persist revenue records in Firestore.

contexts.name: Cost Forecasting
contexts.description: Project future spend from observed usage: forecasting model in core, forecast endpoints, persistence of forecast snapshots, and rendered forecast tables for operators.

contexts.name: Billing Integrations (Stripe/Polar)
contexts.description: Turn external billing events into revenue: a provider registry verifies signed Stripe/Polar webhooks (HMAC) and maps them to revenue records, so margin tracking stays in sync with real customer billing. The runner can also drive billing sync.

contexts.name: Revenue & Margin Tracking
contexts.description: Profit tracking on top of observability: record revenue events (manual or billing-synced) per customer/product, then roll up margin as revenue minus LLM cost, broken down by customer or product over a time window.

contexts.name: Model Pricing & Cost Rollup
contexts.description: The DB-backed pricing book: per provider/model token prices seeded from pricing.json, then editable via API. Generation cost for providers that return no dollar amount is computed from the book by tokens, and usage is rolled up by project/provider/model.

contexts.name: Rust Client SDK
contexts.description: The public Rust client library users embed to send traces to LightTrack, plus the quickstart example that doubles as the onboarding path.

contexts.name: MCP Resources & Error Mapping
contexts.description: MCP server surface beyond tools: expose LightTrack data as MCP resources, define write-tool JSON schemas, and map internal errors onto MCP-shaped error responses.

contexts.name: Responder Investigation & Actions
contexts.description: Business end of the responder: run Claude Code read-only against the mapped local repo to diagnose a spike, guard it with a circuit breaker and git checks, then report out via email or follow-on actions.

contexts.name: Auto-Responder Pipeline
contexts.description: Reactive loop entry: receive an error_spike alert webhook from LightTrack, load responder config, classify whether the failure is actionable (skipping transient/provider errors), enrich it with recent failing events, and drive the investigation pipeline.

contexts.name: Device Agent (lt-agent)
contexts.description: Device half of the relay: lease due tasks from one or more cloud LightTrack instances, execute each against a local action library via the Claude Code CLI, push results into originating apps through per-action connectors, and settle back to the cloud.

contexts.name: Relay Task Leasing (Cloud Side)
contexts.description: Cloud half of the device relay: model relay tasks, lease them to authenticated devices over outbound HTTPS, accept settlement of results, and persist task state in SQLite and Postgres.

contexts.name: Collective API & Rendering
contexts.description: Serving surface for collective intelligence: endpoints that expose aggregated cross-project stats, SQLite persistence of collective rollups, and the rendered comparison views.

contexts.name: Collective Intelligence Core
contexts.description: Cross-tenant aggregation model: classify and alias comparable models/providers across projects, then merge their observations into collective benchmarks without leaking per-tenant detail.

contexts.name: Markdown Render Layer
contexts.description: The shared presentation layer: a Value-in / Markdown-out dispatcher with aligned GFM tables, sparklines, and status glyphs. Both the MCP server and the lt CLI feed raw API JSON through it so humans get the same compact tables everywhere.

contexts.name: Operator CLI & Worker Runtime
contexts.description: The two binaries operators run: the lt CLI (a thin HTTP client over the API for management and reads) and the lt-runner worker shell (clap arg parsing, HTTP client, shared helpers, and claude.exe resolution) that hosts the score/bench/dataset/serve commands.

contexts.name: MCP Server
contexts.description: Expose LightTrack to Claude Code / agents over the Model Context Protocol (JSON-RPC on stdio). Read tools are side-effect-free; write tools stay gated behind an env flag. It is a thin HTTP client of the API (never touches the DB) and ships pre-built prompt journeys as slash commands.

contexts.name: Store Codec, Usage Cache & Limit Persistence
contexts.description: Shared persistence plumbing under the store trait: value codec for serializing records, a cached usage rollup to keep hot limit checks off the main tables, and SQLite persistence of limit rules.

contexts.name: Cloud Store Backends (Postgres + Firestore)
contexts.description: The cloud persistence backends behind the same Store trait, selected by LIGHTTRACK_DATABASE_URL: Postgres (the cross-cloud default, e.g. Neon) and a GCP-native Firestore backend with its REST client and typed-value codec. Each runs the shared conformance suite.

contexts.name: Store Trait & SQLite Engine
contexts.description: The backend-agnostic Store trait plus the local SQLite implementation (connection plumbing, shared row helpers) and the backend-agnostic conformance suite that holds every backend to identical round-trip behavior.

contexts.name: Platform Core & API Server
contexts.description: The pure I/O-free domain model root (shared ids, errors, re-exports) plus the Axum API wiring: main builds the store and price book, assembles the router, and serves. Shared plumbing covers app state, error mapping, auth mode, and request guards.

instructions: Context map for LightTrack. Each group is a business domain (domain field); each context is a feature with its files (filePaths) + a technical category. Read this to learn which files belong to which feature BEFORE editing. When you change a context's files, update them here (or run Vibeman's refresh) to keep this fresh.
